name: Units of Measure

laws: Arithmetic respects dimensional algebra (m/s × s = m)
Adding incompatible units is a type error
Unit annotations are erased at runtime (zero cost)
x.encoding: Native in F#; elsewhere via phantom-type wrappers (C++ <chrono>/Boost.Units, Rust uom, Java/C# libraries) that encode dimensions in the type parameters.

def: Type-level tracking of physical (or domain) units so that dimensionally inconsistent arithmetic — adding metres to seconds — is rejected at compile time.
problem: Mixing quantities of different units (the classic Mars Climate Orbiter failure) is invisible to ordinary numeric types.
mechanism: Each quantity carries its dimension in its type; operations combine or constrain dimensions per dimensional algebra. Mismatches fail to type-check, and the units are erased so there is no runtime cost.
benefits: Eliminates a whole class of unit bugs at compile time
failures: Bypassing the wrappers with raw numbers
Wrong conversion factors inside the type-safe shell
whenNot: No dimensional quantities in the domain
myths: "Naming variables with units is enough" — only the type system actually enforces it
